audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Zophar's first speech: accusation of many words, claim that Job receives less than guilt deserves, unreachable divine wisdom, call to repent, and restoration promise. Marked as Zophar's speech, not final theology.

verses.restored: Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:
Should a multitude of words go unanswered, and should a man of lips be justified?
Should your empty talk make people silent, and when you mock, shall no one shame you?
For you say, My teaching is pure, and I am clean in your eyes.
But if only the Divine would speak and open his lips with you,
and tell you secrets of wisdom, for sound wisdom is double-sided. Know then that the Divine lets some of your guilt be forgotten for you.
Can you find out the deep things of the Divine? Can you find out the limit of Shaddai?
It is higher than heavens; what can you do? Deeper than Sheol; what can you know?
Its measure is longer than the earth and broader than the sea.
If he passes through, imprisons, and calls an assembly, who can turn him back?
For he knows worthless people; he sees harm, and will he not consider it?
An empty-headed person may get heart, when a wild donkey's colt is born human.
If you prepare your heart and stretch out your hands toward him,
if harm is in your hand, put it far away, and do not let injustice dwell in your tents,
then you will lift your face without blemish; you will be firm and not fear.
For you will forget trouble; like waters that have passed by, you will remember it.
Your life will rise brighter than noon; darkness will become like morning.
You will be secure because there is hope; you will look around and lie down in safety.
You will lie down, and none will make you tremble; many will seek your favor.
But the eyes of the wicked will fail; escape will perish from them, and their hope will be the breathing out of life.
